Class CrateService

java.lang.Object
net.sylphian.minecraft.crates.service.CrateService
public class CrateService extends Object
Service responsible for rolling and granting crate rewards.

Provides weighted random rolling via rollRewards(CrateConfig) and rollOne(CrateConfig), item building, and reward granting. The caller is responsible for determining how rolled rewards are presented based on the crate's OpeningStyle.

  • Constructor Details

    • CrateService

      public CrateService()
      Constructs a new CrateService.

  • Method Details

    • rollOne

      public RewardEntry rollOne(CrateConfig crate)
      Rolls a single reward from the crate's weighted pool.
      Parameters:
      crate - the crate to roll from
      Returns:
      the rolled RewardEntry

    • rollRewards

      public List<RewardEntry> rollRewards(CrateConfig crate)
      Rolls rewards from the crate's pool according to CrateConfig.totalRolls(). Uses weighted random selection — entries with higher chances appear more frequently.
      Parameters:
      crate - the crate configuration to roll from
      Returns:
      the list of rolled RewardEntry objects

    • giveReward

      public void giveReward(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, RewardEntry reward)
      Grants a single reward to the player. The item is added to the player's inventory, or dropped at their feet if full.
      Parameters:
      player - the player to reward
      reward - the reward to grant

    • giveAll

      public void giveAll(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, List<RewardEntry> rewards)
      Grants all rewards in the provided list to the player directly. Intended for cases where CrateConfig.playerPicks() equals or exceeds CrateConfig.totalRolls().
      Parameters:
      player - the player to reward
      rewards - the rewards to grant

    • buildItem

      public org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack buildItem(RewardEntry reward)
      Builds the ItemStack for a reward. For external item rewards, the item is resolved from the ItemRegistry and cloned with the configured amount applied. Falls back to a named paper placeholder if the registry lookup fails. For standard rewards, the item is built from the display material, name, lore, and enchantments.
      Parameters:
      reward - the reward to build an item for
      Returns:
      the built ItemStack
